     8→import { ProviderComposer } from '~/components/common/ProviderComposer'
     9→import { useStateToRef } from '~/hooks/common/use-state-ref'
    10→import { clsxm } from '~/lib/helper'
    11→
    12→import { usePageScrollDirection } from '../root/page-scroll-info-provider'
    13→
    14→const [
    15→  WrappedElementProviderInternal,
    16→  useWrappedElement,
    17→  useSetWrappedElement,
    18→] = createContextState<HTMLDivElement | null>(undefined as any)
    19→
    20→const [
    21→  ElementSizeProviderInternal,
    22→  useWrappedElementSize,
    23→  useSetWrappedElementSize,
    24→] = createContextState({
    25→  h: 0,
    26→  w: 0,
    27→})
    28→
    29→const [
    30→  ElementPositionProviderInternal,
    31→  useWrappedElementPosition,
    32→  useSetElementPosition,
    33→] = createContextState({
    34→  x: 0,
    35→  y: 0,
    36→})
    37→
    38→const [
    39→  IsEOArticleElementProviderInternal,
    40→  useIsEoFWrappedElement,
    41→  useSetIsEOArticleElement,
    42→] = createContextState<boolean>(false)
    43→
    44→const Providers = [
    45→  <WrappedElementProviderInternal key="ArticleElementProviderInternal" />,
    46→  <ElementSizeProviderInternal key="ElementSizeProviderInternal" />,
    47→  <ElementPositionProviderInternal key="ElementPositionProviderInternal" />,
    48→  <IsEOArticleElementProviderInternal key="IsEOArticleElementProviderInternal" />,
    49→]
    50→
    51→interface WrappedElementProviderProps {
    52→  eoaDetect?: boolean
    53→  as?: keyof React.JSX.IntrinsicElements
    54→  style?: React.CSSProperties
    55→}
    56→
    57→export const WrappedElementProvider: Component<WrappedElementProviderProps> = ({
    58→  children,
    59→  className,
    60→  style,
    61→  ...props
    62→}) => (
    63→  <ProviderComposer contexts={Providers}>
    64→    <ElementResizeObserver />
    65→    <Content {...props} className={className} style={style}>
    66→      {children}
    67→    </Content>
    68→  </ProviderComposer>
    69→)
    70→const ElementResizeObserver = () => {
    71→  const setSize = useSetWrappedElementSize()
    72→  const setPos = useSetElementPosition()
    73→  const $element = useWrappedElement()
    74→  useIsomorphicLayoutEffect(() => {
    75→    if (!$element) return
    76→    const { height, width, left, top } = $element.getBoundingClientRect()
    77→    setSize({ h: height, w: width })
    78→
    79→    const pageX = window.scrollX + left
    80→    const pageY = window.scrollY + top
    81→    setPos({ x: pageX, y: pageY })
    82→
    83→    const observer = new ResizeObserver((entries) => {
    84→      const entry = entries[0]
    85→
    86→      const { height, width } = entry.contentRect
    87→      const { left, top } = $element.getBoundingClientRect()
    88→      const pageX = window.scrollX + left
    89→      const pageY = window.scrollY + top
    90→
    91→      setSize((size) => {
    92→        if (size.h === height && size.w === width) return size
    93→        return { h: height, w: width }
    94→      })
    95→      setPos((pos) => {
    96→        if (pos.x === pageX && pos.y === pageY) return pos
    97→        return { x: pageX, y: pageY }
    98→      })
    99→    })
   100→    observer.observe($element)
   101→    return () => {
   102→      observer.unobserve($element)
   103→      observer.disconnect()
   104→    }
   105→  }, [$element])
   106→
   107→  return null
   108→}
   109→
   110→const Content: Component<WrappedElementProviderProps> = memo(
   111→  ({ children, className, style, eoaDetect, as = 'div' }) => {
   112→    const setElement = useSetWrappedElement()
   113→
   114→    const As = as as any
   115→    return (
   116→      <As
   117→        className={clsxm('relative', className)}
   118→        style={style}
   119→        ref={setElement}
   120→      >
   121→        {children}
   122→        {eoaDetect && <EOADetector />}
   123→      </As>
   124→    )
   125→  },
   126→)
   127→
   128→Content.displayName = 'ArticleElementProviderContent'
   129→
   130→const EOADetector: Component = () => {
   131→  const dir = usePageScrollDirection()
   132→  const getDir = useStateToRef(dir)
   133→  const setter = useSetIsEOArticleElement()
   134→  const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
   135→  useEffect(() => {
   136→    if (!ref.current) return
   137→    const $el = ref.current
   138→    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
   139→      (entries) => {
   140→        const entry = entries[0]
   141→
   142→        if (!entry.isIntersecting && getDir.current === 'down') {
   143→          return
   144→        }
   145→
   146→        setter(entry.isIntersecting)
   147→      },
   148→      {
   149→        rootMargin: '0px 0px 0px 0px',
   150→      },
   151→    )
   152→
   153→    observer.observe($el)
   154→    return () => {
   155→      observer.unobserve($el)
   156→      observer.disconnect()
   157→    }
   158→  }, [])
   159→
   160→  return <div ref={ref} />
   161→}
   162→
   163→export {
   164→  useIsEoFWrappedElement,
   165→  useSetWrappedElement,
   166→  useWrappedElement,
   167→  useWrappedElementPosition,
   168→  useWrappedElementSize,
   169→}
   170→

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document with all the files and their content:

## TOC Active State Logic - Complete Analysis

Here's a comprehensive report of all files related to the TOC (Table of Contents) active state logic in the Shiroi project:

### File Structure Overview

```
<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/toc/
├── TocHeadingStrategy.tsx         # Strategy pattern for heading queries
├── TocAside.tsx                   # Main TOC container component
├── TocItem.tsx                    # Individual TOC item component
├── TocTree.tsx                    # TOC tree with active state management
├── TocAutoScroll.tsx              # Auto-scroll handler (disabled)
├── TocFAB.tsx                     # Floating Action Button for mobile
└── index.ts                       # Public exports
```

---

### 1. **TocHeadingStrategy.tsx**
**Purpose**: Defines heading query strategies for different content formats.

**Key Components**:
- `HeadingQueryStrategy`: Type for heading query functions
- `markdownHeadingStrategy`: Filters headings with `data-markdown-heading="true"`
- `lexicalHeadingStrategy`: Selects all H1-H6 headings
- `TocHeadingStrategyContext`: React Context for strategy injection
- `useTocHeadingStrategy()`: Hook to access the current strategy
- `TocHeadingStrategyProvider`: Provider component that selects strategy based on `contentFormat`

**Active State Determination**: None (this just provides heading selection)

---

### 2. **TocTree.tsx** 
**Purpose**: Core TOC tree component with Intersection Observer-based active state management.

**Key Components**:
- `tocActiveIdAtom`: Jotai atom storing current active heading ID
- `useActiveId()`: Custom hook implementing the **Intersection Observer pattern**
  - Creates IntersectionObserver with `rootMargin: -100px 0px -100px 0px`
  - Fires when heading enters viewport with bottom/top padding of 100px
  - Updates `activeId` in startTransition for smooth updates
- `TocTree`: Main component that:
  - Calls `useActiveId($headings)` to track active heading
  - Builds TOC structure from headings array
  - Renders `MemoedItem` components with `isActive={heading.anchorId === activeId}`
  - Handles scroll-to-anchor on item click via `handleScrollTo()`

**Active State Determination**: **Intersection Observer** with 100px top/bottom margins

**Scroll Handling**:
- `handleScrollTo()`: Uses `springScrollToElement($el, -100)` to scroll to heading with offset
- `MemoedItem`: Auto-scrolls active TOC item into view (centered in container)

---

### 3. **TocItem.tsx**
**Purpose**: Individual TOC item component with styling based on active state.

**Key Features**:
- Receives `active: boolean` prop
- Applies `active` variant when active (adds left margin, opacity 100)
- Smooth scrolls into TOC list view when active (one-time on mount)
- Styled with tailwind-variants: base opacity 50%, hover opacity 80%, active opacity 100%

---

### 4. **TocAside.tsx**
**Purpose**: Container component for the TOC sidebar.

**Key Features**:
- Uses `WrappedElementProvider` to get article container reference
- Calls `useTocHeadingStrategy()` to get heading query function
- Memoizes heading list based on strategy and article element
- Listens for `DOMCustomEvents.RefreshToc` to force re-render
- Throttles window resize events to update max-width
- Renders `TocTree` with collected headings

---

### 5. **TocFAB.tsx**
**Purpose**: Mobile floating action button that shows TOC in a modal.

**Key Features**:
- Uses `useTocHeadingStrategy()` to query headings
- Renders TOC in modal with `scrollInNextTick={true}` (defers scroll to next frame)
- Dismisses modal on item click

---

### 6. **TocAutoScroll.tsx**
**Purpose**: (Currently disabled) Would handle initial hash-based scroll to heading.

---

### 7. **index.ts**
**Purpose**: Public API exports for the TOC module.

---

## How TocHeadingStrategy is Consumed

### Provider Pattern (Top-Level):
Files that wrap content with `TocHeadingStrategyProvider`:

1. **`/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/posts/(post-detail)/[category]/[slug]/page.tsx`**
   ```tsx
   <TocHeadingStrategyProvider
     contentFormat={data.contentFormat}
     content={data.content}
   >
     {/* Post content and TocFAB */}
   </TocHeadingStrategyProvider>
   ```

2. **`/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/notes/[id]/page.tsx`**
   ```tsx
   <TocHeadingStrategyProvider
     contentFormat={data.data.contentFormat}
     content={data.data.content}
   >
     {/* Note content and TocFAB */}
   </TocHeadingStrategyProvider>
   ```

3. **`/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/(page-detail)/[slug]/layout.tsx`**
   ```tsx
   <TocHeadingStrategyProvider
     contentFormat={data.contentFormat}
     content={data.content}
   >
     {/* Page content and TocFAB */}
   </TocHeadingStrategyProvider>
   ```

### Consumer Pattern (Components):
- **`TocAside`**: Calls `useTocHeadingStrategy()` to query headings from article
- **`TocFAB`**: Calls `useTocHeadingStrategy()` to query headings for modal

---

## Active State Logic Summary

**Method**: Intersection Observer with scroll spy

**Flow**:
1. `useActiveId()` in `TocTree` creates IntersectionObserver
2. Observes all heading elements with `rootMargin: -100px 0px -100px 0px`
3. When heading intersects, updates `tocActiveIdAtom` with heading ID
4. `TocTree` compares `heading.anchorId === activeId` to determine active state
5. Active item gets visual indicator (left border, opacity 100)
6. Active item auto-scrolls into TOC list center
7. User clicks TOC item → `handleScrollTo()` → smooth scroll to heading → manual `setActiveId`

**Key margin**: 100px top and bottom means a heading is considered "active" when it's between the top 100px and bottom 100px of the viewport

### `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/toc/TocHeadingStrategy.tsx`

```tsx
'use client'

import type { PropsWithChildren } from 'react'
import { createContext, use, useMemo } from 'react'

export type HeadingQueryStrategy = (
  container: HTMLElement,
) => HTMLHeadingElement[]

export const markdownHeadingStrategy: HeadingQueryStrategy = (container) => {
  return [...container.querySelectorAll('h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6')].filter(
    ($h) => ($h as HTMLElement).dataset['markdownHeading'] === 'true',
  ) as HTMLHeadingElement[]
}

export const lexicalHeadingStrategy: HeadingQueryStrategy = (container) => {
  return [
    ...container.querySelectorAll('h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6'),
  ] as HTMLHeadingElement[]
}

const TocHeadingStrategyContext = createContext<HeadingQueryStrategy>(
  markdownHeadingStrategy,
)

export const useTocHeadingStrategy = () => use(TocHeadingStrategyContext)

export function TocHeadingStrategyProvider({
  contentFormat,
  content,
  children,
}: PropsWithChildren<{ contentFormat?: string; content?: string | null }>) {
  const strategy = useMemo(
    () =>
      contentFormat === 'lexical' && content
        ? lexicalHeadingStrategy
        : markdownHeadingStrategy,
    [contentFormat, content],
  )
  return (
    <TocHeadingStrategyContext.Provider value={strategy}>
      {children}
    </TocHeadingStrategyContext.Provider>
  )
}
```

```tsx
'use client'

import { m } from 'motion/react'
import type * as React from 'react'
import {
  startTransition,
  useEffect,
  useImperativeHandle,
  useMemo,
  useRef,
} from 'react'

import { useIsImmersiveReadingEnabled } from '~/atoms/hooks/reading'
import { DOMCustomEvents } from '~/constants/event'
import { Spring } from '~/constants/spring'
import { useForceUpdate } from '~/hooks/common/use-force-update'
import { clsxm } from '~/lib/helper'
import { throttle } from '~/lib/lodash'
import { useWrappedElement } from '~/providers/shared/WrappedElementProvider'

import { useTocHeadingStrategy } from './TocHeadingStrategy'
import { TocTree } from './TocTree'

export type TocAsideProps = {
  treeClassName?: string
}

export interface TocSharedProps {
  accessory?: React.ReactNode | React.FC

  as?: React.ElementType
}
export interface TocAsideRef {
  getContainer: () => HTMLUListElement | null
}
export const TocAside = ({
  ref,
  className,
  children,
  treeClassName,
  accessory,
  as: As = 'aside',
}: TocAsideProps &
  TocSharedProps &
  ComponentType & { ref?: React.RefObject<TocAsideRef | null> }) => {
  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLUListElement>(null)
  const $article = useWrappedElement()
  const isImmersive = useIsImmersiveReadingEnabled()

  const [forceUpdate, updated] = useForceUpdate()

  useEffect(() => {
    const handler = () => {
      startTransition(() => {
        forceUpdate()
      })
    }
    document.addEventListener(DOMCustomEvents.RefreshToc, handler)
    return () => {
      document.removeEventListener(DOMCustomEvents.RefreshToc, handler)
    }
  }, [])

  useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({
    getContainer: () => containerRef.current,
  }))

  if ($article === undefined) {
    throw new TypeError('<Toc /> must be used in <WrappedElementProvider />')
  }
  const queryHeadings = useTocHeadingStrategy()
  const $headings = useMemo(() => {
    if (!$article) {
      return []
    }
    return queryHeadings($article)
  }, [$article, updated, queryHeadings])

  useEffect(() => {
    const setMaxWidth = throttle(() => {
      if (containerRef.current) {
        containerRef.current.style.maxWidth = `${
          window.innerWidth -
          containerRef.current.getBoundingClientRect().x -
          30
        }px`
      }
    }, 14)
    setMaxWidth()

    window.addEventListener('resize', setMaxWidth)
    return () => {
      window.removeEventListener('resize', setMaxWidth)
    }
  }, [])

  return (
    <m.aside
      className={clsxm('st-toc z-[3]', 'relative font-sans', className)}
      animate={{
        opacity: isImmersive ? 0.2 : 1,
      }}
      transition={Spring.presets.smooth}
      style={{
        pointerEvents: isImmersive ? 'none' : 'auto',
      }}
    >
      <TocTree
        $headings={$headings}
        containerRef={containerRef}
        className={clsxm('absolute max-h-[75vh]', treeClassName)}
        accessory={accessory}
      />
      {children}
    </m.aside>
  )
}
TocAside.displayName = 'TocAside'
```

```tsx
'use client'

import type { FC, MouseEvent } from 'react'
import { memo, useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useRef } from 'react'
import { tv } from 'tailwind-variants'

import { clsxm } from '~/lib/helper'

const styles = tv({
  base: clsxm(
    'relative mb-[1.5px] inline-block min-w-0 max-w-full leading-normal text-neutral',
    'truncate text-left tabular-nums opacity-50 transition-all duration-500 hover:opacity-80',
  ),
  variants: {
    status: {
      active: 'ml-2 opacity-100',
    },
  },
})
export interface ITocItem {
  depth: number
  title: string
  anchorId: string
  index: number

  $heading: HTMLHeadingElement
}

export const TocItem: FC<{
  heading: ITocItem

  active: boolean
  rootDepth: number
  onClick?: (i: number, $el: HTMLElement | null, anchorId: string) => void
}> = memo((props) => {
  const { active, rootDepth, onClick, heading } = props
  const { $heading, anchorId, depth, index, title } = heading

  const $ref = useRef<HTMLAnchorElement>(null)

  useEffect(() => {
    if (active) {
      $ref.current?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' })
    }
  }, [])

  const renderDepth = useMemo(() => {
    const result = depth - rootDepth

    return result
  }, [depth, rootDepth])

  return (
    <a
      ref={$ref}
      data-index={index}
      href={`#${anchorId}`}
      className={clsxm(
        styles({
          status: active ? 'active' : undefined,
        }),
      )}
      style={useMemo(
        () => ({
          paddingLeft:
            depth >= rootDepth ? `${renderDepth * 0.6 + 0.5}rem` : '0.5rem',
        }),
        [depth, renderDepth, rootDepth],
      )}
      data-depth={depth}
      onClick={useCallback(
        (e: MouseEvent) => {
          e.preventDefault()

          onClick?.(index, $heading, anchorId)
        },
        [onClick, index, $heading, anchorId],
      )}
      title={title}
    >
      <span className="cursor-pointer">{title}</span>
    </a>
  )
})

TocItem.displayName = 'TocItem'
```

### `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/toc/TocTree.tsx`

```tsx
'use client'

import clsx from 'clsx'
import { atom, useAtom } from 'jotai'
import { m } from 'motion/react'
import type { FC } from 'react'
import * as React from 'react'
import {
  memo,
  startTransition,
  useCallback,
  useEffect,
  useMemo,
  useRef,
} from 'react'

import { Divider } from '~/components/ui/divider'
import { RightToLeftTransitionView } from '~/components/ui/transition'
import { useStateToRef } from '~/hooks/common/use-state-ref'
import { useContainerCanScroll } from '~/hooks/shared/use-mask-scrollarea'
import { clsxm } from '~/lib/helper'
import { springScrollToElement } from '~/lib/scroller'

import type { TocSharedProps } from './TocAside'
import type { ITocItem } from './TocItem'
import { TocItem } from './TocItem'

const tocActiveIdAtom = atom<string | null>(null)

function useActiveId($headings: HTMLHeadingElement[]) {
  const [activeId, setActiveId] = useAtom(tocActiveIdAtom)
  useEffect(() => {
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
      (entries) => {
        entries.forEach((entry) => {
          if (entry.isIntersecting) {
            startTransition(() => {
              setActiveId(entry.target.id)
            })
          }
        })
      },
      { rootMargin: `-100px 0px -100px 0px` },
    )
    $headings.forEach(($heading) => {
      observer.observe($heading)
    })
    return () => {
      observer.disconnect()
    }
  }, [$headings])

  return [activeId, setActiveId] as const
}

export const TocTree: Component<
  {
    $headings: HTMLHeadingElement[]
    containerRef?: React.MutableRefObject<HTMLUListElement | null>
    /**
     * 下次循环中跳转，在 Modal 中很管用
     */
    scrollInNextTick?: boolean

    onItemClick?: (item: ITocItem) => void
  } & TocSharedProps
> = ({
  $headings,
  containerRef,
  className,
  accessory,
  scrollInNextTick,
  onItemClick,
}) => {
  const [activeId, setActiveId] = useActiveId($headings)

  const toc: ITocItem[] = useMemo(
    () =>
      Array.from($headings).map((el, idx) => {
        const depth = +el.tagName.slice(1)
        const elClone = el.cloneNode(true) as HTMLElement
        elClone.querySelectorAll('del, .katex-container').forEach((del) => {
          del.remove()
        })

        const title = elClone.textContent || ''

        const index = idx

        return {
          depth,
          index: Number.isNaN(index) ? -1 : index,
          title,
          anchorId: el.id,
          $heading: el,
        }
      }),
    [$headings],
  )

  const rootDepth = useMemo(
    () =>
      toc?.length
        ? (toc.reduce(
            (d: number, cur) => Math.min(d, cur.depth),
            toc[0]?.depth || 0,
          ) as any as number)
        : 0,
    [toc],
  )

  const tocRef = useStateToRef(toc)
  const handleScrollTo = useCallback(
    (i: number, $el: HTMLElement | null, anchorId: string) => {
      onItemClick?.(tocRef.current[i])

      if ($el) {
        const handle = () => {
          springScrollToElement($el, -100).then(() => {
            setActiveId?.(anchorId)
          })
        }
        if (scrollInNextTick) {
          requestAnimationFrame(() => {
            handle()
          })
        } else handle()
      }
    },
    [],
  )
  const accessoryElement = useMemo(() => {
    if (!accessory) return null
    return React.isValidElement(accessory)
      ? accessory
      : React.createElement(accessory as FC)
  }, [accessory])
  const treeRef = useRef<HTMLUListElement>(null)
  const [, canScroll] = useContainerCanScroll({ ref: treeRef })
  return (
    <ul
      className={clsxm(
        'scrollbar-none flex grow flex-col scroll-smooth px-2',
        className,
      )}
      ref={containerRef}
    >
      <ul
        ref={treeRef}
        className={clsx(
          'scrollbar-none overflow-auto',
          canScroll && 'mask-scroller',
        )}
      >
        {toc?.map((heading) => (
          <MemoedItem
            heading={heading}
            isActive={heading.anchorId === activeId}
            key={`${heading.title}-${heading.index}`}
            rootDepth={rootDepth}
            onClick={handleScrollTo}
          />
        ))}
      </ul>
      {accessoryElement && (
        <li className="shrink-0">
          {toc.length > 0 && <Divider />}
          {accessoryElement}
        </li>
      )}
    </ul>
  )
}
const MemoedItem = memo<{
  isActive: boolean
  heading: ITocItem
  rootDepth: number
  onClick?: (i: number, $el: HTMLElement | null, anchorId: string) => void
}>(({ 
  heading,
  isActive,
  onClick,
  rootDepth,
}) => {
  const itemRef = useRef<HTMLElement>(null)

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!isActive) return

    const $item = itemRef.current
    if (!$item) return
    const $container = $item.parentElement
    if (!$container) return

    // 把当前 active Item 滚动到容器的中间
    const containerHeight = $container.clientHeight
    const itemHeight = $item.clientHeight
    const itemOffsetTop = $item.offsetTop
    const { scrollTop } = $container

    const itemTop = itemOffsetTop - scrollTop
    const itemBottom = itemTop + itemHeight
    if (itemTop < 0 || itemBottom > containerHeight) {
      $container.scrollTop =
        itemOffsetTop - containerHeight / 2 + itemHeight / 2
    }
  }, [isActive])

  return (
    <RightToLeftTransitionView
      timeout={
        useRef({
          exit: 50,
          enter: 50 * heading.index,
        }).current
      }
      key={heading.title}
      as="li"
      className="relative leading-none"
      ref={itemRef}
    >
      {isActive && (
        <m.span
          layoutId="active-toc-item"
          layout
          className="absolute inset-y-[3px] left-0 w-[2px] rounded-xs bg-accent"
        />
      )}
      <TocItem
        heading={heading}
        onClick={onClick}
        active={isActive}
        key={heading.title}
        rootDepth={rootDepth}
      />
    </RightToLeftTransitionView>
  )
})
MemoedItem.displayName = 'MemoedItem'
```

```tsx
'use client'

import { useParams } from 'next/navigation'
import { useTranslations } from 'next-intl'
import { useCallback, useMemo } from 'react'

import { FABPortable } from '~/components/ui/fab'
import { useModalStack } from '~/components/ui/modal'
import { MAIN_MARKDOWN_ID } from '~/constants/dom-id'
import { usePathname } from '~/i18n/navigation'

import { useTocHeadingStrategy } from './TocHeadingStrategy'
import { TocTree } from './TocTree'

export const TocFAB = () => {
  const t = useTranslations('common')
  const { present } = useModalStack()
  const pathname = usePathname()
  const params = useParams()

  const queryHeadings = useTocHeadingStrategy()
  const $headings = useMemo(() => {
    const $mainMarkdownRender = document.getElementById(MAIN_MARKDOWN_ID)
    if (!$mainMarkdownRender) return

    return queryHeadings($mainMarkdownRender)
  }, [queryHeadings])
  const presentToc = useCallback(() => {
    present({
      title: t('toc_title'),
      clickOutsideToDismiss: true,
      content: ({ dismiss }) => (
        <TocTree
          $headings={$headings!}
          className="max-h-full space-y-3 overflow-y-auto [&>li]:py-1"
          onItemClick={() => {
            dismiss()
          }}
          scrollInNextTick
        />
      ),
    })
  }, [pathname, params, $headings, present, t])

  if (!$headings?.length) return null

  return (
    <FABPortable aria-label={t('aria_toc')} onClick={presentToc}>
      <i className="i-mingcute-list-expansion-line" />
    </FABPortable>
  )
}
```

Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me create a summary of my findings.

## Summary: Heading Node Rendering in Shiroi's Static Renderer

### Overview

The static renderer (ShiroRenderer) does **NOT** use the `HeadingAnchorPlugin`. Instead, it handles heading ID assignment and anchor generation directly in the static rendering pipeline using a separate implementation.

### Key Components

#### 1. **HeadingAnchorPlugin** (Editor-only)
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/HeadingAnchorPlugin.tsx`

This plugin runs **only in the editor** (RichEditor component) and:
- Listens to mutations on `HeadingNode` using `editor.registerMutationListener(HeadingNode, ...)`
- Extracts text content from heading elements
- Converts text to slugs using `textToSlug()` function
- Deduplicates slugs if multiple headings have the same text
- Sets the heading DOM element's `id` attribute to the generated slug
- Prepends an anchor element with class `rich-heading-anchor` for visual anchor links

**Note**: This plugin is NOT registered in `RichEditor.tsx` - it's available for export but not used by default.

#### 2. **Static Heading Rendering** (Static renderer)
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-renderer/src/engine/renderBuiltinNode.tsx`

The static renderer uses `renderBuiltinNode()` function which handles heading nodes separately (lines 37-64):

```typescript
case 'heading': {
  const Tag = node.tag as 'h1' | 'h2' | 'h3' | 'h4' | 'h5' | 'h6'
  const text = extractText(node)
  const baseSlug = textToSlug(text)
  let slug = baseSlug
  if (baseSlug) {
    const count = headingSlugs.get(baseSlug)
    if (count !== undefined) {
      slug = `${baseSlug}-${count}`
      headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, count + 1)
    } else {
      headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, 1)
    }
  }
  return (
    <Tag key={key} id={slug || undefined} className={`rich-heading-${Tag}`}>
      {slug && (
        <a
          className="rich-heading-anchor"
          aria-hidden="true"
          tabIndex={-1}
          href={`#${slug}`}
        />
      )}
      {children}
    </Tag>
  )
}
```

### Slug Generation Algorithm

Both implementations use the same `textToSlug()` function with identical logic:

**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/HeadingAnchorPlugin.tsx` (lines 5-15) and `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-renderer/src/engine/renderBuiltinNode.tsx` (lines 3-10)

```typescript
function textToSlug(text: string): string {
  return (
    text
      .toLowerCase()
      .trim()
      // Remove non-word characters, CJK characters, and certain punctuation
      .replaceAll(/[^\w\s\u3000-\u9fff\uac00-\ud7af\uff00-\uffef-]/g, '')
      // Replace whitespace and underscores with hyphens
      .replaceAll(/[\s_]+/g, '-')
      // Remove leading/trailing hyphens
      .replaceAll(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
  )
}
```

**Slight difference**: The static renderer version uses `\u3001-\u9fff` while the plugin uses `\u3000-\u9fff`, but the impact is minimal.

### ID Assignment Process

1. **Text Extraction**: Uses `extractText()` to recursively collect all text from the heading node
2. **Slug Generation**: Converts text to slug using `textToSlug()`
3. **Deduplication**: Tracks used slugs in a `Map<string, number>()`:
   - First occurrence: slug as-is, map value = 1
   - Subsequent occurrences: `${slug}-${count}`, map value incremented
4. **Rendering**:
   - Sets `id` attribute on heading element to the generated slug
   - Prepends an anchor link with `href="#${slug}"` if slug is non-empty
   - Anchor has `aria-hidden="true"` and `tabIndex={-1}`

### Rendering Flow

**Static Renderer Entry Point**: `ShiroRenderer` (`<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-kit-shiro/src/ShiroRenderer.tsx`)
- Wraps `RichRenderer` from `@haklex/rich-renderer` package
- Uses `enhancedRendererConfig` from `@haklex/rich-renderers`
- Does not include `HeadingAnchorPlugin`

**Render Pipeline**:
1. Web component calls `ShiroRenderer` (in `LexicalContent.tsx`)
2. `ShiroRenderer` → `RichRenderer` (from `@haklex/rich-renderer`)
3. `RichRenderer` creates headless editor and parses serialized editor state
4. For each heading node, `renderBuiltinNode()` generates slug and renders `<h1-h6>` with `id` and anchor

### CSS Classes

Generated heading elements have class names: `rich-heading-h1`, `rich-heading-h2`, etc.

Anchor elements have class name: `rich-heading-anchor`

     4→
     5→function textToSlug(text: string): string {
     6→  return (
     7→    text
     8→      .toLowerCase()
     9→      .trim()
    10→      // eslint-disable-next-line regexp/no-dupe-characters-character-class
    11→      .replaceAll(/[^\w\s\u3000-\u9fff\uac00-\ud7af\uff00-\uffef-]/g, '')
    12→      .replaceAll(/[\s_]+/g, '-')
    13→      .replaceAll(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
    14→  )
    15→}
    16→
    17→function deduplicateSlug(slug: string, usedSlugs: Map<string, number>): string {
    18→  const count = usedSlugs.get(slug)
    19→  if (count === undefined) {
    20→    usedSlugs.set(slug, 1)
    21→    return slug
    22→  }
    23→  usedSlugs.set(slug, count + 1)
    24→  return `${slug}-${count}`
    25→}
    26→
    27→export function HeadingAnchorPlugin() {
    28→  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
    29→
    30→  useEffect(() => {
    31→    const usedSlugs = new Map<string, number>()
    32→
    33→    return editor.registerMutationListener(HeadingNode, (mutations) => {
    34→      usedSlugs.clear()
    35→
    36→      for (const [nodeKey, mutation] of mutations) {
    37→        if (mutation === 'destroyed') continue
    38→
    39→        const dom = editor.getElementByKey(nodeKey)
    40→        if (!dom) continue
    41→
    42→        const text = dom.textContent || ''
    43→        const baseSlug = textToSlug(text)
    44→        if (!baseSlug) continue
    45→
    46→        const slug = deduplicateSlug(baseSlug, usedSlugs)
    47→        dom.id = slug
    48→
    49→        let anchor = dom.querySelector<HTMLAnchorElement>(
    50→          '.rich-heading-anchor',
    51→        )
    52→        if (!anchor) {
    53→          anchor = document.createElement('a')
    54→          anchor.className = 'rich-heading-anchor'
    55→          anchor.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')
    56→          anchor.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1')
    57→          dom.prepend(anchor)
    58→        }
    59→        anchor.href = `#${slug}`
    60→      }
    61→    })
    62→  }, [editor])
    63→
    64→  return null
    65→}
    66→

```text
renderBuiltinNode.tsx

renderTextNode.tsx
import {
  allNodes,
  ColorSchemeProvider,
  editorTheme,
  FootnoteDefinitionsProvider,
  getVariantClass,
  NestedContentRendererProvider,
  RendererConfigProvider,
} from '@haklex/rich-editor/static'
import { PortalThemeProvider } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
import { createHeadlessEditor } from '@lexical/headless'
import type { LexicalEditor, SerializedEditorState } from 'lexical'
import { $getRoot } from 'lexical'
import { createElement, type ReactNode } from 'react'

import { renderBuiltinNode } from './engine/renderBuiltinNode'
import { renderTextNode } from './engine/renderTextNode'
import { preprocessFootnotes } from './preprocess/footnote'
import type { RichRendererProps } from './types'

interface EditorConfig {
  namespace: string
  theme: Record<string, any>
}

function wrapDecoration(
  serialized: any,
  key: string,
  decoration: ReactNode,
): ReactNode {
  switch (serialized.type) {
    case 'alert-quote':
      return createElement(
        'div',
        { key, className: `rich-alert rich-alert-${serialized.alertType}` },
        decoration,
      )
    case 'banner':
      return createElement(
        'div',
        { key, className: `rich-banner rich-banner-${serialized.bannerType}` },
        decoration,
      )
    case 'grid-container':
      return createElement(
        'div',
        { key, className: 'rich-grid-container' },
        decoration,
      )
    default:
      return decoration
  }
}

function renderTree(
  node: any,
  editor: LexicalEditor,
  editorConfig: EditorConfig,
  headingSlugs: Map<string, number>,
  key: string,
): ReactNode {
  const nodeKey = node.getKey ? node.getKey() : key

  if (typeof node.decorate === 'function') {
    try {
      const decoration = node.decorate(editor, editorConfig)
      if (decoration != null) {
        const serialized = node.exportJSON ? node.exportJSON() : {}
        return wrapDecoration(serialized, nodeKey, decoration)
      }
    } catch {
      /* fallthrough to builtin */
    }
  }

  const serialized = node.exportJSON ? node.exportJSON() : {}

  if (serialized.type === 'text') {
    return renderTextNode(serialized, nodeKey)
  }

  let children: ReactNode[] | null = null
  if (node.getChildren) {
    const childNodes = node.getChildren()
    if (childNodes.length > 0) {
      children = childNodes.map((child: any, i: number) =>
        renderTree(
          child,
          editor,
          editorConfig,
          headingSlugs,
          `${nodeKey}-${i}`,
        ),
      )
    }
  }

  return renderBuiltinNode(serialized, nodeKey, children, headingSlugs)
}

function renderEditorToReact(
  value: SerializedEditorState,
  nodes: any[],
): {
  content: ReactNode
  footnoteData: ReturnType<typeof preprocessFootnotes>
  renderNestedContent: (state: SerializedEditorState) => ReactNode
} {
  const editor = createHeadlessEditor({
    nodes,
    theme: editorTheme,
    editable: false,
    onError: (error: Error) => {
      console.error('[RichRenderer]', error)
    },
  })

  const editorConfig: EditorConfig = { namespace: 'ssr', theme: editorTheme }

  const editorState = editor.parseEditorState(value)
  editor.setEditorState(editorState)

  const footnoteData = preprocessFootnotes(value)

  let content: ReactNode = null
  editorState.read(() => {
    const root = $getRoot()
    const headingSlugs = new Map<string, number>()
    const children = root
      .getChildren()
      .map((child: any, i: number) =>
        renderTree(child, editor, editorConfig, headingSlugs, `ssr-${i}`),
      )
    content = <>{children}</>
  })

  const renderNestedContent = (state: SerializedEditorState): ReactNode => {
    const nestedEditor = createHeadlessEditor({
      nodes,
      theme: editorTheme,
      editable: false,
      onError: (error: Error) => {
        console.error('[RichRenderer:nested]', error)
      },
    })
    const nestedEditorConfig: EditorConfig = {
      namespace: 'ssr-nested',
      theme: editorTheme,
    }
    const nestedState = nestedEditor.parseEditorState(state)
    nestedEditor.setEditorState(nestedState)
    let nested: ReactNode = null
    nestedState.read(() => {
      const root = $getRoot()
      const headingSlugs = new Map<string, number>()
      const ch = root
        .getChildren()
        .map((child: any, i: number) =>
          renderTree(
            child,
            nestedEditor,
            nestedEditorConfig,
            headingSlugs,
            `nested-${i}`,
          ),
        )
      nested = <>{ch}</>
    })
    return nested
  }

  return { content, footnoteData, renderNestedContent }
}

export function RichRenderer({
  value,
  variant = 'article',
  theme = 'light',
  className,
  style,
  as: Component = 'div',
  rendererConfig,
  extraNodes,
}: RichRendererProps) {
  const variantClass = getVariantClass(variant, theme)
  const nodes = extraNodes ? [...allNodes, ...extraNodes] : allNodes

  const { content, footnoteData, renderNestedContent } = renderEditorToReact(
    value,
    nodes,
  )

  const classes = ['rich-content', variantClass, className]
    .filter(Boolean)
    .join(' ')

  return (
    <PortalThemeProvider className={variantClass}>
      <ColorSchemeProvider colorScheme={theme}>
        <RendererConfigProvider
          config={rendererConfig}
          mode="renderer"
          variant={variant}
        >
          <FootnoteDefinitionsProvider
            definitions={footnoteData.definitions}
            displayNumberMap={footnoteData.displayNumberMap}
          >
            <NestedContentRendererProvider value={renderNestedContent}>
              <Component className={classes} style={style} data-theme={theme}>
                {content}
              </Component>
            </NestedContentRendererProvider>
          </FootnoteDefinitionsProvider>
        </RendererConfigProvider>
      </ColorSchemeProvider>
    </PortalThemeProvider>
  )
}
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
     2→
     3→function textToSlug(text: string): string {
     4→  return text
     5→    .toLowerCase()
     6→    .trim()
     7→    .replaceAll(/[^\w\s\u3001-\u9fff\uac00-\ud7af\uff00-\uffef-]/g, '')
     8→    .replaceAll(/[\s_]+/g, '-')
     9→    .replaceAll(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
    10→}
    11→
    12→function extractText(node: any): string {
    13→  if (node.text) return node.text
    14→  if (node.children) return node.children.map(extractText).join('')
    15→  return ''
    16→}
    17→
    18→export function renderBuiltinNode(
    19→  node: any,
    20→  key: string,
    21→  children: ReactNode[] | null,
    22→  headingSlugs: Map<string, number>,
    23→): ReactNode {
    24→  switch (node.type) {
    25→    case 'root':
    26→      return <>{children}</>
    27→    case 'paragraph': {
    28→      const align = node.format
    29→        ? ({ textAlign: node.format } as const)
    30→        : undefined
    31→      return (
    32→        <p key={key} className="rich-paragraph" style={align}>
    33→          {children}
    34→        </p>
    35→      )
    36→    }
    37→    case 'heading': {
    38→      const Tag = node.tag as 'h1' | 'h2' | 'h3' | 'h4' | 'h5' | 'h6'
    39→      const text = extractText(node)
    40→      const baseSlug = textToSlug(text)
    41→      let slug = baseSlug
    42→      if (baseSlug) {
    43→        const count = headingSlugs.get(baseSlug)
    44→        if (count !== undefined) {
    45→          slug = `${baseSlug}-${count}`
    46→          headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, count + 1)
    47→        } else {
    48→          headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, 1)
    49→        }
    50→      }
    51→      return (
    52→        <Tag key={key} id={slug || undefined} className={`rich-heading-${Tag}`}>
    53→          {slug && (
    54→            <a
    55→              className="rich-heading-anchor"
    56→              aria-hidden="true"
    57→              tabIndex={-1}
    58→              href={`#${slug}`}
    59→            />
    60→          )}
    61→          {children}
    62→        </Tag>
    63→      )
    64→    }
    65→    case 'quote':
    66→      return (
    67→        <blockquote key={key} className="rich-quote">
    68→          {children}
    69→        </blockquote>
    70→      )
    71→    case 'list': {
    72→      const Tag = node.listType === 'number' ? 'ol' : 'ul'
    73→      const cls =
    74→        node.listType === 'number'
    75→          ? 'rich-list-ol'
    76→          : node.listType === 'check'
    77→            ? 'rich-checklist rich-list-ul'
    78→            : 'rich-list-ul'
    79→      return (
    80→        <Tag
    81→          key={key}
    82→          className={cls}
    83→          start={node.start !== 1 ? node.start : undefined}
    84→        >
    85→          {children}
    86→        </Tag>
    87→      )
    88→    }
    89→    case 'listitem': {
    90→      const isChecklist = node.checked !== undefined
    91→      const hasNestedList = node.children?.some((c: any) => c.type === 'list')
    92→      let cls: string
    93→      if (hasNestedList) {
    94→        cls = 'rich-list-nested-item'
    95→      } else if (isChecklist) {
    96→        cls = node.checked
    97→          ? 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-checked'
    98→          : 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-unchecked'
    99→      } else {
   100→        cls = 'rich-list-item'
   101→      }
   102→      return (
   103→        <li key={key} className={cls} value={node.value}>
   104→          {children}
   105→        </li>
   106→      )
   107→    }
   108→    case 'link':
   109→      return (
   110→        <a
   111→          key={key}
   112→          className="rich-link"
   113→          href={node.url}
   114→          target={node.target}
   115→          rel={node.rel}
   116→        >
   117→          {children}
   118→        </a>
   119→      )
   120→    case 'autolink':
   121→      return (
   122→        <a
   123→          key={key}
   124→          className="rich-link"
   125→          href={node.url}
   126→          target="_blank"
   127→          rel="noopener noreferrer"
   128→        >
   129→          {children}
   130→        </a>
   131→      )
   132→    case 'horizontalrule':
   133→      return <hr key={key} className="rich-hr" />
   134→    case 'table':
   135→      return (
   136→        <div key={key} className="rich-table-scrollable-wrapper">
   137→          <table className="rich-table">{children}</table>
   138→        </div>
   139→      )
   140→    case 'tablerow':
   141→      return <tr key={key}>{children}</tr>
   142→    case 'tablecell': {
   143→      const CellTag = node.headerState ? 'th' : 'td'
   144→      const cls = node.headerState
   145→        ? 'rich-table-cell-header'
   146→        : 'rich-table-cell'
   147→      return (
   148→        <CellTag
   149→          key={key}
   150→          className={cls}
   151→          colSpan={node.colSpan > 1 ? node.colSpan : undefined}
   152→        >
   153→          {children}
   154→        </CellTag>
   155→      )
   156→    }
   157→    case 'details': {
   158→      const summary = node.summary || ''
   159→      return (
   160→        <details
   161→          key={key}
   162→          className="rich-details"
   163→          open={node.open || undefined}
   164→        >
   165→          <summary className="rich-details-summary">
   166→            <span className="rich-details-summary-text">{summary}</span>
   167→            <span className="rich-details-chevron">
   168→              <svg
   169→                width="20"
   170→                height="20"
   171→                viewBox="0 0 20 20"
   172→                fill="none"
   173→                stroke="currentColor"
   174→                strokeWidth="1.5"
   175→                strokeLinecap="round"
   176→                strokeLinejoin="round"
   177→              >
   178→                <path d="M6 8L10 12L14 8" />
   179→              </svg>
   180→            </span>
   181→          </summary>
   182→          <div className="rich-details-content">{children}</div>
   183→        </details>
   184→      )
   185→    }
   186→    case 'spoiler':
   187→      return (
   188→        <span key={key} className="rich-spoiler" role="button" tabIndex={0}>
   189→          {children}
   190→        </span>
   191→      )
   192→    case 'code':
   193→      return (
   194→        <pre key={key} className="rich-code-block">
   195→          <code>{children}</code>
   196→        </pre>
   197→      )
   198→    case 'code-highlight':
   199→      return <span key={key}>{node.text}</span>
   200→    case 'linebreak':
   201→      return <br key={key} />
   202→    case 'tab':
   203→      return <span key={key}>{'  '}</span>
   204→    default:
   205→      return null
   206→  }
   207→}
   208→
```

    18→// Mirror @theme inline font values into shadow root (Tailwind v4 @theme inline does not emit CSS vars)
    19→const fontVarsCss = `:host{--font-sans:var(--app-font-sans),system-ui,-apple-system,'PingFang SC','Microsoft YaHei','Segoe UI',Roboto,Helvetica,'noto sans sc','hiragino sans gb','sans-serif',Apple Color Emoji,Segoe UI Emoji,Not Color Emoji;--font-serif:'Noto Serif CJK SC','Noto Serif SC',var(--app-font-serif),'Source Han Serif SC','Source Han Serif',source-han-serif-sc,SongTi SC,SimSum,'Hiragino Sans GB',system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,Roboto,Helvetica,'Microsoft YaHei','WenQuanYi Micro Hei',sans-serif;--font-mono:'OperatorMonoSSmLig Nerd Font','Cascadia Code PL','FantasqueSansMono Nerd Font','operator mono',JetBrainsMono,'Fira code Retina','Fira code','Consolas',Monaco,'Hannotate SC',monospace,-apple-system}`
    20→
    21→const linkCardFetchContext: LinkCardFetchContext = {
    22→  adapters: {
    23→    github: {
    24→      request: fetchGitHubApi,
    25→    },
    26→    tmdb: {
    27→      request: (url) => {
    28→        const u = new URL(url)
    29→        const path = u.pathname.replace(/^\/3\//, '')
    30→        return fetch(`/api/tmdb/${path}${u.search}`).then((r) => r.json())
    31→      },
    32→    },
    33→    bangumi: {
    34→      request: (url) => {
    35→        const u = new URL(url)
    36→        const path = u.pathname.replace(/^\/v0\//, '')
    37→        return fetch(`/api/bangumi/${path}`).then((r) => r.json())
    38→      },
    39→    },
    40→    leetcode: {
    41→      request: (_url, init) => {
    42→        return fetch('/api/leetcode', init).then((r) => r.json())
    43→      },
    44→    },
    45→    'netease-music': {
    46→      request: (url) => {
    47→        const songId = new URL(url).pathname.split('/').pop()
    48→        return fetch('/api/music/netease', {
    49→          method: 'POST',
    50→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    51→          body: JSON.stringify({ songId }),
    52→        }).then((r) => r.json())
    53→      },
    54→    },
    55→    'qq-music': {
    56→      request: (url) => {
    57→        const songId = new URL(url).pathname.split('/').pop()
    58→        return fetch('/api/music/tencent', {
    59→          method: 'POST',
    60→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    61→          body: JSON.stringify({ songId }),
    62→        }).then((r) => r.json())
    63→      },
    64→    },
    65→    'mx-space': {
    66→      request: (path) => {
    67→        return fetch(`/api/v2/${path}`).then((r) => r.json())
    68→      },
    69→    },
    70→  },
    71→}
    72→
    73→export interface LexicalContentProps {
    74→  content: string
    75→  variant?: RichEditorVariant
    76→  className?: string
    77→}
    78→
    79→export function LexicalContent({
    80→  content,
    81→  variant,
    82→  className,
    83→}: LexicalContentProps) {
    84→  const editorState = useMemo<SerializedEditorState | null>(() => {
    85→    try {
    86→      return JSON.parse(content)
    87→    } catch {
    88→      return null
    89→    }
    90→  }, [content])
    91→
    92→  const isDark = useIsDark()
    93→
    94→  const editorOverrideStyle = useMemo(
    95→    () =>
    96→      createThemeStyle({
    97→        color: {
    98→          accent: 'var(--color-accent)',
    99→          link: 'var(--color-accent)',
   100→          accentLight:
   101→            'color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-accent) 20%, transparent)',
   102→          quoteBorder: 'var(--color-accent)',
   103→        },
   104→        layout: { maxWidth: '100%' },
   105→        typography: {
   106→          fontFamily:
   107→            variant === 'note'
   108→              ? 'var(--note-font-override, var(--font-serif))'
   109→              : 'var(--font-sans)',
   110→          fontFamilySerif: 'var(--font-serif)',
   111→          fontMono: 'var(--font-mono)',
   112→        },
   113→      }),
   114→    [variant],
   115→  )
   116→
   117→  if (!editorState) return null
   118→
   119→  return (
   120→    <LinkCardFetchProvider value={linkCardFetchContext}>
   121→      <ShiroRenderer
   122→        value={editorState}
   123→        variant={variant}
   124→        theme={isDark ? 'dark' : 'light'}
   125→        className={clsx(className, 'bg-transparent!')}
   126→        style={editorOverrideStyle}
   127→      />
   128→    </LinkCardFetchProvider>
   129→  )
   130→}
   131→

     8→  TldrawNode,
     9→} from '@haklex/rich-renderers'
    10→import type { Klass, LexicalNode } from 'lexical'
    11→import { useMemo } from 'react'
    12→
    13→const defaultExtraNodes = [
    14→  TldrawNode,
    15→  ...embedNodes,
    16→  ...galleryNodes,
    17→  ...codeSnippetNodes,
    18→]
    19→
    20→export interface ShiroRendererProps extends Omit<
    21→  RichRendererProps,
    22→  'rendererConfig' | 'extraNodes'
    23→> {
    24→  extraNodes?: Array<Klass<LexicalNode>>
    25→}
    26→
    27→export function ShiroRenderer({ extraNodes, ...props }: ShiroRendererProps) {
    28→  const mergedNodes = useMemo(
    29→    () =>
    30→      extraNodes ? [...defaultExtraNodes, ...extraNodes] : defaultExtraNodes,
    31→    [extraNodes],
    32→  )
    33→
    34→  return (
    35→    <RichRenderer
    36→      {...props}
    37→      extraNodes={mergedNodes}
    38→      rendererConfig={enhancedRendererConfig}
    39→    />
    40→  )
    41→}

Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me create a summary of my findings.

## Summary: Heading Node Rendering in Shiroi's Static Renderer

### Overview

The static renderer (ShiroRenderer) does **NOT** use the `HeadingAnchorPlugin`. Instead, it handles heading ID assignment and anchor generation directly in the static rendering pipeline using a separate implementation.

### Key Components

#### 1. **HeadingAnchorPlugin** (Editor-only)
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/HeadingAnchorPlugin.tsx`

This plugin runs **only in the editor** (RichEditor component) and:
- Listens to mutations on `HeadingNode` using `editor.registerMutationListener(HeadingNode, ...)`
- Extracts text content from heading elements
- Converts text to slugs using `textToSlug()` function
- Deduplicates slugs if multiple headings have the same text
- Sets the heading DOM element's `id` attribute to the generated slug
- Prepends an anchor element with class `rich-heading-anchor` for visual anchor links

**Note**: This plugin is NOT registered in `RichEditor.tsx` - it's available for export but not used by default.

#### 2. **Static Heading Rendering** (Static renderer)
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-renderer/src/engine/renderBuiltinNode.tsx`

The static renderer uses `renderBuiltinNode()` function which handles heading nodes separately (lines 37-64):

```typescript
case 'heading': {
  const Tag = node.tag as 'h1' | 'h2' | 'h3' | 'h4' | 'h5' | 'h6'
  const text = extractText(node)
  const baseSlug = textToSlug(text)
  let slug = baseSlug
  if (baseSlug) {
    const count = headingSlugs.get(baseSlug)
    if (count !== undefined) {
      slug = `${baseSlug}-${count}`
      headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, count + 1)
    } else {
      headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, 1)
    }
  }
  return (
    <Tag key={key} id={slug || undefined} className={`rich-heading-${Tag}`}>
      {slug && (
        <a
          className="rich-heading-anchor"
          aria-hidden="true"
          tabIndex={-1}
          href={`#${slug}`}
        />
      )}
      {children}
    </Tag>
  )
}
```

### Slug Generation Algorithm

Both implementations use the same `textToSlug()` function with identical logic:

**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/HeadingAnchorPlugin.tsx` (lines 5-15) and `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-renderer/src/engine/renderBuiltinNode.tsx` (lines 3-10)

```typescript
function textToSlug(text: string): string {
  return (
    text
      .toLowerCase()
      .trim()
      // Remove non-word characters, CJK characters, and certain punctuation
      .replaceAll(/[^\w\s\u3000-\u9fff\uac00-\ud7af\uff00-\uffef-]/g, '')
      // Replace whitespace and underscores with hyphens
      .replaceAll(/[\s_]+/g, '-')
      // Remove leading/trailing hyphens
      .replaceAll(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
  )
}
```

**Slight difference**: The static renderer version uses `\u3001-\u9fff` while the plugin uses `\u3000-\u9fff`, but the impact is minimal.

### ID Assignment Process

1. **Text Extraction**: Uses `extractText()` to recursively collect all text from the heading node
2. **Slug Generation**: Converts text to slug using `textToSlug()`
3. **Deduplication**: Tracks used slugs in a `Map<string, number>()`:
   - First occurrence: slug as-is, map value = 1
   - Subsequent occurrences: `${slug}-${count}`, map value incremented
4. **Rendering**:
   - Sets `id` attribute on heading element to the generated slug
   - Prepends an anchor link with `href="#${slug}"` if slug is non-empty
   - Anchor has `aria-hidden="true"` and `tabIndex={-1}`

### Rendering Flow

**Static Renderer Entry Point**: `ShiroRenderer` (`<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-kit-shiro/src/ShiroRenderer.tsx`)
- Wraps `RichRenderer` from `@haklex/rich-renderer` package
- Uses `enhancedRendererConfig` from `@haklex/rich-renderers`
- Does not include `HeadingAnchorPlugin`

**Render Pipeline**:
1. Web component calls `ShiroRenderer` (in `LexicalContent.tsx`)
2. `ShiroRenderer` → `RichRenderer` (from `@haklex/rich-renderer`)
3. `RichRenderer` creates headless editor and parses serialized editor state
4. For each heading node, `renderBuiltinNode()` generates slug and renders `<h1-h6>` with `id` and anchor

### CSS Classes

Generated heading elements have class names: `rich-heading-h1`, `rich-heading-h2`, etc.

Anchor elements have class name: `rich-heading-anchor`

    27→  serialized: any,
    28→  key: string,
    29→  decoration: ReactNode,
    30→): ReactNode {
    31→  switch (serialized.type) {
    32→    case 'alert-quote':
    33→      return createElement(
    34→        'div',
    35→        { key, className: `rich-alert rich-alert-${serialized.alertType}` },
    36→        decoration,
    37→      )
    38→    case 'banner':
    39→      return createElement(
    40→        'div',
    41→        { key, className: `rich-banner rich-banner-${serialized.bannerType}` },
    42→        decoration,
    43→      )
    44→    case 'grid-container':
    45→      return createElement(
    46→        'div',
    47→        { key, className: 'rich-grid-container' },
    48→        decoration,
    49→      )
    50→    default:
    51→      return decoration
    52→  }
    53→}
    54→
    55→function renderTree(
    56→  node: any,
    57→  editor: LexicalEditor,
    58→  editorConfig: EditorConfig,
    59→  headingSlugs: Map<string, number>,
    60→  key: string,
    61→): ReactNode {
    62→  const nodeKey = node.getKey ? node.getKey() : key
    63→
    64→  if (typeof node.decorate === 'function') {
    65→    try {
    66→      const decoration = node.decorate(editor, editorConfig)
    67→      if (decoration != null) {
    68→        const serialized = node.exportJSON ? node.exportJSON() : {}
    69→        return wrapDecoration(serialized, nodeKey, decoration)
    70→      }
    71→    } catch {
    72→      /* fallthrough to builtin */
    73→    }
    74→  }
    75→
    76→  const serialized = node.exportJSON ? node.exportJSON() : {}
    77→
    78→  if (serialized.type === 'text') {
    79→    return renderTextNode(serialized, nodeKey)
    80→  }
    81→
    82→  let children: ReactNode[] | null = null
    83→  if (node.getChildren) {
    84→    const childNodes = node.getChildren()
    85→    if (childNodes.length > 0) {
    86→      children = childNodes.map((child: any, i: number) =>
    87→        renderTree(
    88→          child,
    89→          editor,
    90→          editorConfig,
    91→          headingSlugs,
    92→          `${nodeKey}-${i}`,
    93→        ),
    94→      )
    95→    }
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  return renderBuiltinNode(serialized, nodeKey, children, headingSlugs)
    99→}
   100→
   101→function renderEditorToReact(
   102→  value: SerializedEditorState,
   103→  nodes: any[],
   104→): {
   105→  content: ReactNode
   106→  footnoteData: ReturnType<typeof preprocessFootnotes>
   107→  renderNestedContent: (state: SerializedEditorState) => ReactNode
   108→} {
   109→  const editor = createHeadlessEditor({
   110→    nodes,
   111→    theme: editorTheme,
   112→    editable: false,
   113→    onError: (error: Error) => {
   114→      console.error('[RichRenderer]', error)
   115→    },
   116→  })
   117→
   118→  const editorConfig: EditorConfig = { namespace: 'ssr', theme: editorTheme }
   119→
   120→  const editorState = editor.parseEditorState(value)
   121→  editor.setEditorState(editorState)
   122→
   123→  const footnoteData = preprocessFootnotes(value)
   124→
   125→  let content: ReactNode = null
   126→  editorState.read(() => {
   127→    const root = $getRoot()
   128→    const headingSlugs = new Map<string, number>()
   129→    const children = root
   130→      .getChildren()
   131→      .map((child: any, i: number) =>
   132→        renderTree(child, editor, editorConfig, headingSlugs, `ssr-${i}`),
   133→      )
   134→    content = <>{children}</>
   135→  })
   136→
   137→  const renderNestedContent = (state: SerializedEditorState): ReactNode => {
   138→    const nestedEditor = createHeadlessEditor({
   139→      nodes,
   140→      theme: editorTheme,
   141→      editable: false,
   142→      onError: (error: Error) => {
   143→        console.error('[RichRenderer:nested]', error)
   144→      },
   145→    })
   146→    const nestedEditorConfig: EditorConfig = {
   147→      namespace: 'ssr-nested',
   148→      theme: editorTheme,
   149→    }
   150→    const nestedState = nestedEditor.parseEditorState(state)
   151→    nestedEditor.setEditorState(nestedState)
   152→    let nested: ReactNode = null
   153→    nestedState.read(() => {
   154→      const root = $getRoot()
   155→      const headingSlugs = new Map<string, number>()
   156→      const ch = root
   157→        .getChildren()
   158→        .map((child: any, i: number) =>
   159→          renderTree(
   160→            child,
   161→            nestedEditor,
   162→            nestedEditorConfig,
   163→            headingSlugs,
   164→            `nested-${i}`,
   165→          ),
   166→        )
   167→      nested = <>{ch}</>
   168→    })
   169→    return nested
   170→  }
   171→
   172→  return { content, footnoteData, renderNestedContent }
   173→}
   174→
   175→export function RichRenderer({
   176→  value,
   177→  variant = 'article',
   178→  theme = 'light',
   179→  className,
   180→  style,
   181→  as: Component = 'div',
   182→  rendererConfig,
   183→  extraNodes,
   184→}: RichRendererProps) {
   185→  const variantClass = getVariantClass(variant, theme)
   186→  const nodes = extraNodes ? [...allNodes, ...extraNodes] : allNodes
   187→
   188→  const { content, footnoteData, renderNestedContent } = renderEditorToReact(
   189→    value,
   190→    nodes,
   191→  )
   192→
   193→  const classes = ['rich-content', variantClass, className]
   194→    .filter(Boolean)
   195→    .join(' ')
   196→
   197→  return (
   198→    <PortalThemeProvider className={variantClass}>
   199→      <ColorSchemeProvider colorScheme={theme}>
   200→        <RendererConfigProvider
   201→          config={rendererConfig}
   202→          mode="renderer"
   203→          variant={variant}
   204→        >
   205→          <FootnoteDefinitionsProvider
   206→            definitions={footnoteData.definitions}
   207→            displayNumberMap={footnoteData.displayNumberMap}
   208→          >
   209→            <NestedContentRendererProvider value={renderNestedContent}>
   210→              <Component className={classes} style={style} data-theme={theme}>
   211→                {content}
   212→              </Component>
   213→            </NestedContentRendererProvider>
   214→          </FootnoteDefinitionsProvider>
   215→        </RendererConfigProvider>
   216→      </ColorSchemeProvider>
   217→    </PortalThemeProvider>
   218→  )
   219→}
   220→
```
</details></tool-use>

1. `ShiroRenderer` → `RichRenderer`

  <LinkCardFetchProvider>

</article>

Let me now look at the TocTree.tsx more carefully, specifically the `useActiveId` hook and how it interacts with the headings. I need to understand why the active state doesn't work.

Let me re-read the TocTree code and think about the issue.

The key flow:
1. `lexicalHeadingStrategy` queries all h1-h6 from the container
2. These headings have IDs set by `renderBuiltinNode` (e.g., `id={slug || undefined}`)
3. `useActiveId` creates an IntersectionObserver that watches these headings


4. When a heading intersects, `setActiveId(entry.target.id)` is called
5. `isActive={heading.anchorId === activeId}` determines active state

The headings DO have IDs, the IntersectionObserver DOES observe them, and the comparison SHOULD work. But maybe the IntersectionObserver approach itself has a fundamental flaw that was previously hidden by the shadow root rendering—with the shadow DOM, the old strategy found headings inside it, but now I'm wondering if the observer is actually detecting intersections correctly.

The real issue might be that React is recreating the heading elements on re-renders. When `LexicalContent` re-renders due to `isDark` changing or parent updates, React could be reconciling those heading DOM nodes. Even though React typically reuses existing elements when keys and types match, something in the render cycle might be breaking the observer's reference to those elements. The keys should be stable since they're based on index, so React should reuse DOM elements even though `renderEditorToReact` creates new JSX objects on each render. But I'm wondering if the real issue is that the page uses a custom scroll container with `overflow-y: auto` instead of relying on the viewport, which would break the IntersectionObserver.

    70→const ElementResizeObserver = () => {
    71→  const setSize = useSetWrappedElementSize()
    72→  const setPos = useSetElementPosition()
    73→  const $element = useWrappedElement()
    74→  useIsomorphicLayoutEffect(() => {
    75→    if (!$element) return
    76→    const { height, width, left, top } = $element.getBoundingClientRect()
    77→    setSize({ h: height, w: width })
    78→
    79→    const pageX = window.scrollX + left
    80→    const pageY = window.scrollY + top
    81→    setPos({ x: pageX, y: pageY })
    82→
    83→    const observer = new ResizeObserver((entries) => {
    84→      const entry = entries[0]
    85→
    86→      const { height, width } = entry.contentRect
    87→      const { left, top } = $element.getBoundingClientRect()
    88→      const pageX = window.scrollX + left
    89→      const pageY = window.scrollY + top
    90→
    91→      setSize((size) => {
    92→        if (size.h === height && size.w === width) return size
    93→        return { h: height, w: width }

    97→        return { x: pageX, y: pageY }
    98→      })
    99→    })
   100→    observer.observe($element)
   101→    return () => {

   103→      observer.disconnect()
   104→    }
   105→  }, [$element])
   106→
   107→  return null
   108→}

   125→  },

   158→  }, [])

   160→  return <div ref={ref} />
   161→}

Actually, I'm realizing the problem might be with how the IntersectionObserver is configured. The root margin creates a viewport zone that's 100px inset from the top and bottom, so headings only register as active when they're in that middle band of the viewport. When a heading enters this zone, the observer should trigger and update the active state through the atom.

But here's the thing—if you're scrolling fast enough that multiple intersection events get batched together in a single callback, the forEach loop would process all of them in sequence. So even if a heading enters and then immediately leaves the zone, the last event processed determines the final state. Since we only act on `isIntersecting: true`, the heading would stay marked as active as long as that was the last event fired.

The real issue might be what happens on initial page load—the IntersectionObserver fires immediately for all observed elements with their current intersection state, and I need to think through what that means for which heading gets marked as active first.

     5→
     6→export type HeadingQueryStrategy = (
     7→  container: HTMLElement,
     8→) => HTMLHeadingElement[]
     9→
    10→export const markdownHeadingStrategy: HeadingQueryStrategy = (container) => {
    11→  return [...container.querySelectorAll('h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6')].filter(
    12→    ($h) => ($h as HTMLElement).dataset['markdownHeading'] === 'true',
    13→  ) as HTMLHeadingElement[]
    14→}
    15→
    16→export const lexicalHeadingStrategy: HeadingQueryStrategy = (container) => {
    17→  return [
    18→    ...container.querySelectorAll('h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6'),
    19→  ] as HTMLHeadingElement[]
    20→}
    21→
    22→const TocHeadingStrategyContext = createContext<HeadingQueryStrategy>(
    23→  markdownHeadingStrategy,
    24→)
    25→
    26→export const useTocHeadingStrategy = () => use(TocHeadingStrategyContext)
    27→
    28→export function TocHeadingStrategyProvider({
    29→  contentFormat,
    30→  content,
    31→  children,
    32→}: PropsWithChildren<{ contentFormat?: string; content?: string | null }>) {
    33→  const strategy = useMemo(
    34→    () =>
    35→      contentFormat === 'lexical' && content
    36→        ? lexicalHeadingStrategy
    37→        : markdownHeadingStrategy,
    38→    [contentFormat, content],
    39→  )
    40→  return (
    41→    <TocHeadingStrategyContext.Provider value={strategy}>
    42→      {children}
    43→    </TocHeadingStrategyContext.Provider>
    44→  )
    45→}
    46→
```
</details></tool-use>

    27→
    28→const tocActiveIdAtom = atom<string | null>(null)
    29→
    30→function useActiveId($headings: HTMLHeadingElement[]) {
    31→  const [activeId, setActiveId] = useAtom(tocActiveIdAtom)
    32→  useEffect(() => {
    33→    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
    34→      (entries) => {
    35→        entries.forEach((entry) => {
    36→          if (entry.isIntersecting) {
    37→            startTransition(() => {
    38→              setActiveId(entry.target.id)
    39→            })
    40→          }
    41→        })
    42→      },
    43→      { rootMargin: `-100px 0px -100px 0px` },
    44→    )
    45→    $headings.forEach(($heading) => {
    46→      observer.observe($heading)
    47→    })
    48→    return () => {
    49→      observer.disconnect()
    50→    }
    51→  }, [$headings])
    52→
    53→  return [activeId, setActiveId] as const
    54→}
    55→
    56→export const TocTree: Component<

    58→    $headings: HTMLHeadingElement[]
    59→    containerRef?: React.MutableRefObject<HTMLUListElement | null>

    77→  const toc: ITocItem[] = useMemo(
    78→    () =>
    79→      Array.from($headings).map((el, idx) => {
    80→        const depth = +el.tagName.slice(1)
    81→        const elClone = el.cloneNode(true) as HTMLElement
    82→        elClone.querySelectorAll('del, .katex-container').forEach((del) => {
    83→          del.remove()
    84→        })
    85→
    86→        const title = elClone.textContent || ''
    87→
    88→        const index = idx
    89→
    90→        return {
    91→          depth,
    92→          index: Number.isNaN(index) ? -1 : index,
    93→          title,
    94→          anchorId: el.id,
    95→          $heading: el,
    96→        }
    97→      }),
    98→    [$headings],
    99→  )
   100→
   101→  const rootDepth = useMemo(
   102→    () =>
   103→      toc?.length
   104→        ? (toc.reduce(
   105→            (d: number, cur) => Math.min(d, cur.depth),
   106→            toc[0]?.depth || 0,
   107→          ) as any as number)
   108→        : 0,
   109→    [toc],
   110→  )
   111→
   112→  const tocRef = useStateToRef(toc)
   113→  const handleScrollTo = useCallback(
   114→    (i: number, $el: HTMLElement | null, anchorId: string) => {
   115→      onItemClick?.(tocRef.current[i])
   116→
   117→      if ($el) {

   119→          springScrollToElement($el, -100).then(() => {
   120→            setActiveId?.(anchorId)
   121→          })
   122→        }
   123→        if (scrollInNextTick) {
   124→          requestAnimationFrame(() => {
   125→            handle()
   126→          })
   127→        } else handle()
   128→      }
   129→    },
   130→    [],
   131→  )
   132→  const accessoryElement = useMemo(() => {
   133→    if (!accessory) return null
   134→    return React.isValidElement(accessory)
   135→      ? accessory
   136→      : React.createElement(accessory as FC)
   137→  }, [accessory])
   138→  const treeRef = useRef<HTMLUListElement>(null)
   139→  const [, canScroll] = useContainerCanScroll({ ref: treeRef })
   140→  return (
   141→    <ul
   142→      className={clsxm(
   143→        'scrollbar-none flex grow flex-col scroll-smooth px-2',
   144→        className,
   145→      )}
   146→      ref={containerRef}
   147→    >
   148→      <ul
   149→        ref={treeRef}
   150→        className={clsx(
   151→          'scrollbar-none overflow-auto',
   152→          canScroll && 'mask-scroller',
   153→        )}
   154→      >
   155→        {toc?.map((heading) => (
   156→          <MemoedItem
   157→            heading={heading}
   158→            isActive={heading.anchorId === activeId}
   159→            key={`${heading.title}-${heading.index}`}
   160→            rootDepth={rootDepth}
   161→            onClick={handleScrollTo}
   162→          />
   163→        ))}

   165→      {accessoryElement && (

   167→          {toc.length > 0 && <Divider />}
   168→          {accessoryElement}
   169→        </li>
   170→      )}
   171→    </ul>
   172→  )
   173→}
   174→const MemoedItem = memo<{
   175→  isActive: boolean
   176→  heading: ITocItem
   177→  rootDepth: number
   178→  onClick?: (i: number, $el: HTMLElement | null, anchorId: string) => void
   179→}>((props) => {

   182→    isActive,
   183→    onClick,
   184→    rootDepth,

   186→  } = props
   187→
   188→  const itemRef = useRef<HTMLElement>(null)
   189→
   190→  useEffect(() => {
   191→    if (!isActive) return
   192→
   193→    const $item = itemRef.current
   194→    if (!$item) return
   195→    const $container = $item.parentElement
   196→    if (!$container) return
   197→
   198→    // 把当前 active Item 滚动到容器的中间
   199→    const containerHeight = $container.clientHeight
   200→    const itemHeight = $item.clientHeight
   201→    const itemOffsetTop = $item.offsetTop
   202→    const { scrollTop } = $container
   203→
   204→    const itemTop = itemOffsetTop - scrollTop
   205→    const itemBottom = itemTop + itemHeight
   206→    if (itemTop < 0 || itemBottom > containerHeight) {
   207→      $container.scrollTop =
   208→        itemOffsetTop - containerHeight / 2 + itemHeight / 2
   209→    }
   210→  }, [isActive])
   211→
   212→  return (
   213→    <RightToLeftTransitionView
   214→      timeout={
   215→        useRef({

   217→          enter: 50 * heading.index,
   218→        }).current
   219→      }
   220→      key={heading.title}
   221→      as="li"
   222→      className="relative leading-none"
   223→      ref={itemRef}

   228→          layout
   229→          className="absolute inset-y-[3px] left-0 w-[2px] rounded-xs bg-accent"
   230→        />
   231→      )}
   232→      <TocItem

   234→        onClick={onClick}
   235→        active={isActive}
   236→        key={heading.title}
   237→        rootDepth={rootDepth}
   238→      />

   240→  )

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-25 17:20:15Z)**_

两处问题：

1. `lexicalHeadingStrategy` 选了 container 内所有 h1-h6，误选到 `sr-only` 里的 `PostTitle` h1（无 id），导致 `activeId` 被置为空串。

    30→export const throttle = <F extends (...args: any[]) => any>(
    31→  func: F,
    32→  wait: number,
    33→  options: {
    34→    leading?: boolean
    35→    trailing?: boolean
    36→  } = {},
    37→): ((...args: Parameters<F>) => void) => {
    38→  let timeoutId: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | undefined
    39→  let lastArgs: Parameters<F> | undefined
    40→  let lastCallTime: number | undefined
    41→
    42→  const doLater = () => {
    43→    timeoutId = undefined
    44→    if (lastArgs !== undefined) {
    45→      func.apply(this, lastArgs)
    46→      lastArgs = undefined
    47→      lastCallTime = Date.now()
    48→      timeoutId = setTimeout(doLater, wait)
    49→    }
    50→  }
    51→
    52→  return function (this: any, ...args: Parameters<F>) {
    53→    const currentTime = Date.now()
    54→
    55→    if (lastCallTime === undefined && options.leading === false) {
    56→      lastCallTime = currentTime
    57→    }
    58→
    59→    const remainingTime = wait - (currentTime - (lastCallTime ?? 0))
```
